_note: Ready-to-insert interactive_image block for the human eye (horizontal section). Image: eye_illus.webp (keyed transparent + compressed from the founder's generated PNG, 1448x1086 landscape). To ship: upload the webp to R2 via the books asset-upload route, put the returned URL in `src`, and add this block to the target biology page (Class 11 Neural Control and Coordination -- sense organs). Hotspots authored + browser-verified against the image on 2026-07-15 -- all 10 placements confirmed live, including the tightest cluster in the whole library (cornea/iris/pupil/lens/ciliary body, all in the front-of-eye region) with a 5-combo streak and no pill-overlap. COORDINATES CORRECTED 2026-07-15 (f): prepare_illustration.py was fixed to crop transparent canvas padding before resizing (was previously only resizing, leaving unused margin around the subject) -- this image was regenerated tighter, and every hotspot fraction below was recomputed via an exact affine transform (not re-eyeballed) from the old to the new crop box. Re-verified live in the browser after the fix.
caption: 📸 Tap each dot to explore — or hit 'Test yourself' to play Label Sprint

hotspots.label: Cornea
hotspots.detail: The transparent front layer that does most of the eye's light-focusing.

hotspots.label: Iris
hotspots.detail: The coloured muscular ring that controls how much light enters by adjusting the pupil's size.

hotspots.label: Pupil
hotspots.detail: The opening in the centre of the iris through which light enters the eye.

hotspots.label: Lens
hotspots.detail: A transparent biconvex structure that fine-tunes focusing, changing shape via the ciliary muscles (accommodation).

hotspots.label: Ciliary body
hotspots.detail: Holds the lens in place and contains the muscles that change the lens's shape for **accommodation**.

hotspots.label: Sclera
hotspots.detail: The tough white outer coat that gives the eyeball its shape and protects it.

hotspots.label: Choroid
hotspots.detail: A pigmented, blood-vessel-rich layer between the sclera and retina that nourishes the eye and absorbs stray light.

hotspots.label: Retina
hotspots.detail: The innermost light-sensitive layer containing rods and cones, where light is converted into nerve signals.

hotspots.label: Blind spot
hotspots.detail: The point where the optic nerve exits the retina — has **no photoreceptors**, so it can't detect light.

hotspots.label: Optic nerve
hotspots.detail: Carries visual signals from the retina to the brain.

generation_prompt: A clean anatomically accurate educational illustration of a horizontal section of the human eye, standard biology-textbook layout, semi-realistic painterly style. Show clearly separated: cornea, iris, pupil, lens, ciliary body, retina, optic nerve, sclera, choroid, and the blind spot (optic disc) where the nerve exits. Muted palette, flat, solid deep-charcoal background #121316, no text/labels/numbers/leader lines. Landscape 4:3, high resolution.
